Art title 1: Are You Ready Yet? (Worldly wealth holding you back?)
Artist Statement 1: This is a good test of our loyalties: if God were to ask us to give up something, what would be hard for us to give up? There are many good things we don't think God would ask us to give up. But there are also many unhelpful things he wants us to give up. Which, in either category, might hold a more important place in your heart? Which things are holding you back if God were to call you into something different?
Art title 2: ART: Use Your Freedom to Love
Artist Statement 2: If you had the freedom to take what you wanted, would you act for the good of others? Or only for yourself?
excerpt:
I remember C.S. Lewis writing that it doesn't matter too much how much you tithe except that you should be tithing a little bit more than you are comfortable with giving.  

And I've been thinking about that in other aspects of my life. What things are actually a little bit unpleasant because I'm trying to be more holy, or sacrificial, or generous. Or do I do things exactly the way I'd like to?  

I think of this in terms of sexuality. Are there indulgent things I'd like to be doing right now that I am choosing not to?  

And in how I talk to people. How often do I respond to those closest to me in anger or condescension just because I feel like it? How many of those times am I actually halting something from coming out of my mouth?  

Ooh, here's a hard one. Who am I not forgiving because it's just easier to simmer in anger?  

I imagine that if I am not a little bit uncomfortable, I am not growing. C.S. Lewis also wrote a book called The Great Divorce about heaven and hell. And his depiction of hell was a city in which anybody could get whatever they so desired. And what that resulted in was a bunch of diminishing souls living as far apart from others that they needed so their desires wouldn't be encroached upon. 

But in heaven even the grass was so firm and real that it was uncomfortable to walk on for the weak-self souls. But the more the people in heaven moved toward the high mountains, straining to make progress, the stronger and more real and substantial they became. And because they endured through what was uncomfortable they became stronger and holier and more mature, beautiful souls.  

Raw Spoon, 9-3-2020
